front: Recite the Mission of the Navy.
back: To recruit, train, equip, and organize to deliver combat ready Naval forces to win conflicts and wars while maintaining security and deterrence through sustained forward presence.

front: Define Discipline (spell it out).
back: Discipline, D-I-S-C-I-P-L-I-N-E, Discipline is the instant, willing obedience to orders, respect for authority and self-reliance.

front: Navy Core Value — define Honor.
back: I am accountable for my professional and personal behavior. I will be mindful of the privilege I have to serve my fellow Americans.

front: Navy Core Value — define Courage.
back: Courage is the value that gives me the moral and mental strength to do what is right, with confidence and resolution, even in the face of temptation or adversity.

front: Navy Core Value — define Commitment.
back: The day-to-day duty of every man and woman in the Department of the Navy is to join together as a team to improve the quality of our work, our people and ourselves.

front: CNO Core Attribute — define Integrity.
back: Our behaviors as individuals and as an organization align with our values as a profession. We actively strengthen each other's resolve to act consistently with our values. As individuals, as teams, and as a Navy, our conduct must always be upright and honorable both in public and when nobody's looking.

front: CNO Core Attribute — define Accountability.
back: We are a mission-focused force. We achieve and maintain high standards. Our actions support our strategy. We clearly define the problem we're trying to solve and proposed outcomes. In execution, we honestly assess our progress and adjust as required—we are our own toughest critic.

front: CNO Core Attribute — define Initiative.
back: On their own, everybody strives to be the best they can be – we give 100% when on the job. Our leaders take ownership and act to the limit of their authorities. We foster a questioning attitude and look at new ideas with an open mind. Our most junior teammate may have the best idea; we must be open to capturing that idea.

front: CNO Core Attribute — define Toughness.
back: We can take a hit and keep going, tapping all sources of strength and resilience: rigorous training for operations and combat, the fighting spirit of our people, and the steadfast support of our families. We don't give up the ship!

front: How many Articles are in the Code of Conduct, and what is the reference?
Recite Code of Conduct — Article 1.
back: I am an American, fighting in the forces which guard my country and our way of life. I am prepared to give my life in their defense.

front: Recite Code of Conduct — Article 2.
back: I will never surrender of my own free will. If in command, I will never surrender the members of my command while they still have the means to resist.

front: Recite Code of Conduct — Article 3.
back: If I am captured I will continue to resist by all means available. I will make every effort to escape and aid others to escape. I will accept neither parole nor special favors from the enemy.

front: Recite Code of Conduct — Article 4.
back: If I become a prisoner of war, I will keep faith with my fellow prisoners. I will give no information or take part in any action which might be harmful to my comrades. If I am senior, I will take command. If not, I will obey the lawful orders of those appointed over me and will back them up in every way.

front: Recite Code of Conduct — Article 5.
back: When questioned, should I become a prisoner of war, I am required to give name, rank, service number and date of birth. I will evade answering further questions to the utmost of my ability. I will make no oral or written statements disloyal to my country and its allies or harmful to their cause.

front: Recite Code of Conduct — Article 6.
back: I will never forget that I am an American, fighting for freedom, responsible for my actions, and dedicated to the principles which made my country free. I will trust in my God and in the United States of America.

front: When is the birthday of the U.S. Navy?
Leadership Trait — define Judgement.
back: The ability to weigh facts and possible solutions on which to base sound decisions.

front: Leadership Trait — define Justice.
back: Giving reward and punishment according to merits of the case in question; the ability to administer a system of rewards and punishments impartially and consistently.

front: Leadership Trait — define Decisiveness.
back: Ability to make decisions promptly and to announce them in clear, forceful manner.

front: Leadership Trait — define Initiative.
back: Taking action in the absence of orders.

front: Leadership Trait — define Dependability.
back: The certainty of proper performance of duty.

front: Leadership Trait — define Tact.
back: The ability to deal with others without creating offense.

front: Leadership Trait — define Integrity.
back: Uprightness of character and soundness of moral principles; includes the qualities of truthfulness and honesty.

front: Leadership Trait — define Endurance.
back: The mental and physical stamina measured by the ability to withstand pain, fatigue, stress and hardship.

front: Leadership Trait — define Bearing.
back: Creating a favorable impression in carriage, appearance and personal conduct at all times.

front: Leadership Trait — define Unselfishness.
back: Avoidance of providing for one's own comfort and personal advancement at the expense of others.

front: Leadership Trait — define Courage.
back: The mental quality that recognizes fear of danger or criticism, but enables a person to proceed in the face of it with calmness and firmness.

front: Leadership Trait — define Knowledge.
back: Understanding of a science or an art. The range of one's information, including professional knowledge and an understanding of your Sailors.

front: Leadership Trait — define Loyalty.
back: The quality of faithfulness to country, the Navy, the unit, to one's seniors, subordinates and peers.

front: Leadership Trait — define Enthusiasm.
back: The display of sincere interest and exuberance in the performance of duty.

Recite the Oath of Office.
back: I, (name), do solemnly swear (or affirm) that I will support and defend the Constitution of the United States against all enemies, foreign and domestic; that I will bear true faith and allegiance to the same; that I take this obligation freely, without any mental reservation or purpose of evasion; and that I will well and faithfully discharge the duties of the office on which I am about to enter. So help me God.

front: Recite the Sailor's Creed.
back: I am a United States Sailor. I will support and defend the Constitution of the United States of America and I will obey the orders of those appointed over me. I represent the fighting spirit of the Navy and those who have gone before me to defend freedom and democracy around the world. I proudly serve my country's Navy combat team with honor, courage and commitment. I am committed to excellence and the fair treatment of all.

How many General Orders of a Sentry are there?
General Order #1?
back: Take charge of this post and all government property in view.

front: General Order #2?
back: Walk my post in a military manner, keeping always on the alert and observing everything that takes place within sight or hearing.

front: General Order #3?
back: Report all violations of orders I am instructed to enforce.

front: General Order #4?
back: Repeat all calls from any post more distant from the guard house than my own.

front: General Order #5?
back: Quit my post only when properly relieved.

front: General Order #6?
back: Receive, obey, and pass on to the sentry who relieves me all orders from the commanding officer, command duty officer, officer of the deck, and officers and petty officers of the watch only.

front: General Order #7?
back: Talk to no one except in the line of duty.

front: General Order #8?
back: Give the alarm in case of fire or disorder.

front: General Order #9?
back: Call the OOD in any case not covered by instructions.

front: General Order #10?
back: Salute all Officers and all colors and standards not cased.

front: General Order #11?
back: Be especially watchful at night, and during the time for challenging, challenge all persons on or near my post, and allow no one to pass without proper authority.

front: Recite verse 1 of Anchors Aweigh.
back: Stand, Navy, out to sea, Fight our battle cry; We'll never change our course, So vicious foe steer shy. Roll out the TNT, Anchors Aweigh. Sail on to victory And sink their bones to Davy Jones, hooray!

front: Recite verse 2 of Anchors Aweigh.
back: Anchors Aweigh, my boys, Anchors Aweigh. Farewell to foreign shores, we sail at break of day. Through our last night ashore, drink to the foam, Until we meet once more. Here's wishing you a happy voyage home.

front: Recite verse 3 of Anchors Aweigh.
back: Blue of the Mighty Deep; Gold of God's great sun. Let these our colors be till all of time be done. On Seven Seas we learn Navy's stern call: Faith, courage, service true with honor over, honor over all.

front: Recite verse 1 of the Marine Corps Hymn.
back: From the Halls of Montezuma To the shores of Tripoli; We fight our country's battles In the air, on land, and sea; First to fight for right and freedom And to keep our honor clean; We are proud to claim the title Of United States Marine.

front: Recite verse 2 of the Marine Corps Hymn.
back: Our flag's unfurled to every breeze From dawn to setting sun; We have fought in ev'ry clime and place Where we could take a gun; In the snow of far-off Northern lands And in sunny tropic scenes; You will find us always on the job The United States Marines.

front: Recite verse 3 of the Marine Corps Hymn.
back: Here's health to you and to our Corps Which we are proud to serve; In many a strife we've fought for life And never lost our nerve; If the Army and the Navy Ever look on Heaven's scenes; They will find the streets are guarded By United States Marines.

Galley procedures motto (three words required at all times)?
Galley motto — which is NOT part of it?
Who may modify basic galley procedures?
Why may the Class Team modify galley procedures?
Before entering, the company halts at which building?
What is "The Chute"?
back: The blue awning at the front of Ney Hall

front: Where exactly does the company halt at Ney Hall?
back: The step leading to the double door entrance

front: Chow time for each class/company is found where?
Where does the Drill Officer march the company before entering the galley?
back: Front of Ney Hall under the blue awning ("The Chute"), halted at the step to the double doors

front: Where does the Drill Officer post before commencing galley procedures?
back: Port side of the formation, abreast of the first rank, then right face to the company

front: Drill Officer: "Guide, post the guidon." — Guide's response?
After posting the guidon, where does the Guide fall in?
Drill Officer: "Mess Treasurer, fall out." — Mess Treasurer's response?
When the Mess Treasurer falls out, what two things do they do?
back: Report company arrival to the mess attendant and request seating

front: Glow belts during entering: stow in which pocket?
"Prepare to crush starboard" — when do students say "Pain"?
back: When both heels come together after the starboard side-step

text: Drill Officer: "Ready" → company: "{{c1::Discipline}}"; Drill Officer: "Move" → company: "{{c2::Pain}}" (crush starboard).

front: Drill Officer: "Mess Treasurer, Report." — what does the Mess Treasurer report about lines?
back: The company will utilize all lines (1, 2, 3, 4)

front: Mess Treasurer report: seating location is directed by whom?
back: Galley Attendant (goat locker / active scullery / inactive scullery / shark tank)

front: Drill Officer: "First four to the door" — what do the first four do?
back: Open the inner and outer doors to the galley

front: Drill Officer: "Column of files from the left, last Sailor sound off last Sailor." — column leader's response?
Other squad leaders during column of files from the left?

text: Last Sailor in the column sounds off: "{{c1::Last Sailor}}"

front: When entering the galley, where are hand sanitizer dispensers?
back: On the right inside the doors

front: When are covers removed during entry?
back: While filing out, before sanitizing hands

front: In the chow line: talking or looking around?
back: No talking and no looking around

front: Tray position while moving through chow lines?
back: Elbows tucked, arms parallel to the deck

front: Student forgot knowledge book/sheet at chow — what do they study with?
back: A table condiment held straight out in the right hand

front: Outer garments (coats/jackets) at the table — when removed?
back: Before arranging trays; placed on the back of the chair

front: Coffee/salad bar (if authorized): retrieve when?
back: After the 15-minute warning (or at 15-minute warning per class team)

front: Section Leader seat location at the table?
back: Forward-most, inboard to the active scullery

front: Section Leader: "Put it away table one" — table response?
At preparatory command "Take" (before Seats), where is the right hand?
back: On the back of the chair (overhand grip), in unison

text: At execution "Seats," the table recites: "{{c1::TO LEAD IS TO SERVE}} / {{c2::ALWAYS WATCHED ALWAYS JUDGED}} / {{c3::YOUR COMPANY MANTRA}}"

front: Seated at attention: heel angle?
Seated at attention: hand position?
back: Knife hands flat on each lap, not beyond the knees

front: From which side do students move into seats?
15-minute warning: what may students begin?
back: Eating (thousand-yard stare; elbows off table; chew with mouth closed)

front: 10- or 5-minute warnings: company reply if no food in mouth?
Done eating early before warnings end — position?
back: POA, thousand-yard stare, feet 45°, knife hands on upper thighs

front: Immediate warning: does the table respond verbally?
back: No — pause; no one gets up to retrieve anything

front: After immediate warning, cups go where?
"Take … feet" — list three actions before facing inboard.
back: Stand, push chairs in, don outer wear and glow belts, pick up trays, wipe table area

front: Trash vs trays on exit: where does each go?
back: Trash (non-food) in trash cans; trays in the scullery

front: Exit route after scullery?
back: Up the center passageway as directed by the Class Chief

front: Column of files to exit: which side is chosen?
back: Left or right — whichever is closest to the active scullery

front: Company fails commands (not in unison / no motivation) — who may call "As you were"?
Tray arrangement reference?
back: Tray diagrams on page F-4 of the knowledge book

front: Galley entering: after Mess Treasurer returns and centers, next Drill Officer command?
Galley entering: after crush starboard, next step involving Mess Treasurer?
back: Mess Treasurer, Report (after MT centers on company)

front: After "Put it away," next Section Leader command sequence?
After immediate warning, next Section Leader command?
